items.title: Amazon Labour Ruling Tests Cost Assumptions In Fulfilment Network Narrative
items.description: The B.C. Labour Relations Board has ruled that Amazon imposed an unlawful wage freeze on about 800 unionized workers at its Delta, B.C. fulfillment center. The dispute centers on wage adjustments during ongoing contract talks, with negotiations reportedly stalled since December 2025. The union has applied for mediation in an effort to speed up a first collective agreement and resolve the wage issue. For investors watching Amazon.com, NasdaqGS:AMZN, this ruling comes as the share price sits...

items.title: Berkshire Hathaway Takes Stake In New York Times, Cuts Apple, Amazon Holdings
items.description: Berkshire Hathaway late Tuesday disclosed a new position in the New York Times valued at about $350 million.  Warren Buffett's Berkshire reduced positions in Apple, BofA and Amazon.  Late Tuesday, New York Times shares rose 3% to a new high as Berkshire's stake became public.

items.title: Bank of America resets Nvidia stock forecast
items.description: Nvidia (NVDA) stock has gained about 32% over the past year, at the time of writing, Tuesday afternoon, Feb. 17, according to Yahoo Finance. Meanwhile, the SPDR S&P 500 index (SPY) is up about 11% in the same period. It is impressive that Nvidia has outpaced the S&P 500 by more than 20%, ...

items.title: Cadence beats quarterly profit and revenue estimates on strong AI-linked demand
items.description: Cadence Design Systems beat quarterly revenue estimates on Tuesday, as strong demand for complex ‌artificial intelligence processors lifted sales of its chip design ‌software. Shares of the San Jose, California-based company were up nearly 4% in ​extended trading. Elevated demand for chips that can handle complex AI processes has benefited the company, which offers specialized design software for mapping out intricate circuits and blueprints for common components ‌such as memory connectors.
